name: Milk Bread

ingredients.item: of a cup milk that has been evaporated
three c. flour for bread (384 g.)
(56 g) softened butter, diced, plus extra for the
Reserved 1 tbsp. Granulated sugar 1 tbsp. egg

method: To make the tangzhong, mix water, milk, and flour in a small
pot over medium-low heat until entirely smooth. Continue to
boil, frequently whisking, until the mixture has thickened into a
soft, springy paste, about 1 to 2 minutes. Allow cooling to room
temperature before serving.
In the meantime, combine 1/2 cup lukewarm whole milk, 1
tablespoon condensed milk, and yeast in the bowl of a stand
mixer and set aside until frothy, about 15 minutes.
Take 1 tablespoon of the beaten egg and set it aside for the egg
wash. Mix on low speed until a loose dough forms, then add the
remaining beaten egg, the cooled tangzhong, remaining 7
tablespoons condensed milk, flour, evaporated milk, and salt to
the mixer bowl.
Increase the mixer's speed to medium and continue mixing for 3
minutes, or until a smooth dough forms. Distance - vector the
sides of the bowl, then increase the mixer speed to medium-high
for 3 minutes more. Continue to beat on medium-high speed
while gradually adding in the butter. Continue beating on
medium-high speed for another 12 to 15 minutes, scraping
down the sides of the bowl twice more. (Warning: you may
need to hold your mixer in place to keep it from slipping off the
counter!)
Cover the bowl and set aside for 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 45
minutes, or until almost doubled in size.
Knead the dough and turn it out onto a floured board. Make
three equal pieces of dough. Flatten each piece of dough into a
12-inch-thick rectangle with your hands, then press into a letter-
fold: start with the short end, fold the top 13 percent of the
rectangle towards the center, and fold the bottom 13 percent of
the rectangle towards the middle. Next, turn the dough 90
degrees, flatten it out into a huge rectangle once more, and
repeat the letter-folding process. Finally, start rolling each
dough into a tight coil from the shorter side of the rectangle.
Using butter, grease a 9" x 5" loaf pan. Place each piece of
dough seam-side down in the prepared pan and let prove for 1
hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 45 minutes, or until slightly more
than doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit 20 minutes before
baking. In a mixing bowl, whisk together the ingredients for the
egg wash. Egg wash the top of the proofed bread evenly.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and
the internal temperature of the loaf reaches 195°. (After 25
minutes, cover loosely with foil if the top of the loaf is turning
too black.)
Take off the heat in the pan for 10 minutes
before turning it out onto a cooling rack. Allow for at least 1
hour of cooling time before slicing.
